highlight.title: The Rise of Modular Monoliths: Why Google and Others Are Stepping Back from Microservices
highlight.summary: <cite index='11-2'>A 2023 paper titled Towards Modern Development of Cloud Applications, published by a team at Google, once again highlights the pitfalls of microservices architecture and the many benefits of the much maligned and misunderstood monolith.</cite> <cite index='14-5,14-7'>A modular monolith is one deployable application split into real modules aligned to the domain. You get the speed and simplicity of a monolith with the order and evolvability of services—without the tax of distributed systems.</cite> This architectural pattern is gaining traction as a pragmatic middle ground that addresses microservices complexity while maintaining scalability.

resources.title: Understanding Modular Monoliths: A Beginner's Guide
resources.why_now: <cite index='12-4,12-6'>The module in the Modular Monolith is a business module that is able to fully provide a set of desired features. In this way, frequent changes affect only one module - it becomes more independent, autonomous and is able to provide functionality by itself.</cite> Perfect for understanding how to organize complex applications without microservices overhead.

resources.title: Event Sourcing Pattern Explained with Real-World Banking Example
resources.why_now: <cite index='23-1,23-2,23-7'>A real-world example of Event Sourcing can be seen in a modern banking system. The system creates related events whenever a consumer does an action that impacts their account, like making a deposit, taking money out of their account, moving money to another account.</cite> Essential for understanding how financial systems maintain perfect audit trails.

resources.title: Netflix's Architectural Evolution: From Monolith to Microservices
resources.why_now: <cite index='8-9,8-10'>Real-world case studies are a great way to learn about the architecture, design, and scalability of popular tech companies. Dive into these case studies to understand how companies like Twitter, Netflix, Uber and more have evolved over the years.</cite> Learn from actual architectural decisions and trade-offs made at scale.

resources.title: Domain-Driven Design Simplified for Modern Development
resources.why_now: <cite index='33-5,33-6'>In today's fast-paced world of software development, aligning technical implementations with business needs is more important than ever. Domain-Driven Design (DDD) offers a structured way to tackle this challenge by emphasizing the importance of business logic and domain knowledge.</cite> Critical for building software that actually solves business problems rather than technical puzzles.

claude_take: The architecture world is witnessing a fascinating correction: after years of microservices evangelism, even Google is acknowledging the complexity tax of distributed systems. <cite index='19-39,19-40'>Even Google is jumping on board the modular monolith trend in their recent research paper. Here are the five main challenges Google identified with microservices: Performance - The overhead of serializing data and sending it across the network has a noticeable impact on performance.</cite> The smartest teams are now starting with modular monoliths that can evolve into microservices when genuinely needed—not because it's trendy, but because it's pragmatic.
